title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: systemd bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for systemd is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The systemd packages contain systemd, a system and service manager for Linux, compatible with the SysV and LSB init scripts. It provides aggressive parallelism capabilities, uses socket and D-Bus activation for starting services, offers on-demand starting of daemons, and keeps track of processes using Linux cgroups. In addition, it supports snapshotting and restoring of the system state, maintains mount and automount points, and implements an elaborate transactional dependency-based service control logic. It can also work as a drop-in replacement for sysvinit.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): Please own /etc/systemd/network [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-111610)  Getting "Unexpected error response from GetNameOwner(): Connection terminated", causing various services to be stopped unexpectedly [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-111629)

full_text:
An update for systemd is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The systemd packages contain systemd, a system and service manager for Linux, compatible with the SysV and LSB init scripts. It provides aggressive parallelism capabilities, uses socket and D-Bus activation for starting services, offers on-demand starting of daemons, and keeps track of processes using Linux cgroups. In addition, it supports snapshotting and restoring of the system state, maintains mount and automount points, and implements an elaborate transactional dependency-based service control logic. It can also work as a drop-in replacement for sysvinit.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): Please own /etc/systemd/network [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-111610)  Getting "Unexpected error response from GetNameOwner(): Connection terminated", causing various services to be stopped unexpectedly [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-111629)
